scenes.audio_text: I run $2M ARR in e-commerce. No full-time employees. Just me and a $300-a-month stack.
scenes.visual_prompt: Medium close-up of a man in cream linen shirt, seated at a worn wooden table in a corner cafe, golden morning window light from the left, exposed brick wall behind, leaning forward slightly, both hands on the table, looking directly at camera

scenes.audio_text: Eighteen months ago I was paying a $72K ops coordinator. She quit on a Friday. Orders were still coming in.
scenes.visual_prompt: Wide shot on a rooftop terrace at dusk, city skyline in background, blue-hour ambient light, one folding chair and a closed laptop on a concrete ledge, arms loosely crossed, looking at camera

scenes.audio_text: I tried hiring a freelancer. $1,800 later, she missed three supplier deadlines in two weeks. I was back to doing it myself.
scenes.visual_prompt: Over-shoulder shot in a bright home kitchen, white countertop with printed invoice and half-empty coffee mug, midday natural light through a sliding glass door, elbow on knee looking down at phone

scenes.audio_text: So I mapped every task she did. Turns out 80% of it was just moving data. Airtable stores it. Zapier moves it. ChatGPT writes the emails.
scenes.visual_prompt: Medium close-up in a small private studio, leaning against a whiteboard with hand-drawn boxes and arrows in black marker, one hand on chin, single warm overhead light casting defined shadows, slow push-in as posture shifts forward

scenes.audio_text: That stack costs me $300 a month. It handles supplier POs, customer escalations, and inventory alerts. Runs every day, never calls in sick.
scenes.visual_prompt: Extreme wide shot walking along a busy urban street at golden hour, shopfronts glowing, pedestrians moving around, coffee cup in one hand, phone in the other, tracking shot following from the side

scenes.audio_text: You don't need headcount. You need a decision tree that runs without you. That's the whole business.
scenes.visual_prompt: Medium close-up in a quiet city park at dusk, iron bench, fallen leaves, stone fountain in soft focus behind, open hands resting on bench back, half-turned looking directly into camera, very slow handheld drift, calm and resolute

caption:
I run $2M a year with zero employees.

Not because I'm special. Because I stopped treating coordination work like judgment work.

Eighteen months ago, I was paying a $72K/year ops coordinator. She quit on a Friday. Orders kept coming. I panicked, hired a freelancer for $1,800 — she missed three supplier deadlines in two weeks.

That's when I stopped trying to replace a person and started building a system.

Here's what I found: 80% of what my coordinator did was moving data. From an email to a spreadsheet to a Slack message to a supplier portal. That's not thinking. That's routing.

Airtable stores the data. Zapier moves it. ChatGPT drafts the follow-ups. Total: $300/month.

The stack runs 24/7. Never calls in sick. Never needs a raise. Never takes its institutional knowledge to a competitor.

That $72K coordinator role? I don't need it anymore. Not because I work harder — because I made every decision once, locked it into a system, and let the system execute.

⚡ Map every decision your team makes. Then automate the routing. The judgment calls? You keep those.

Save this for the day you think you need a hire.

linkedin.caption:
I run $2M ARR with zero full-time employees.

Not a hot take. Just a decision tree I wrote once and let software execute every day.

Eighteen months ago, my ops coordinator quit on a Friday. I tried replacing her — $1,800 on a freelancer who missed three supplier deadlines in two weeks.

The problem wasn't the person. It was that I was trying to fill a role instead of building a system.

I mapped every task she did. 80% was data routing — moving information from an email to a spreadsheet to a Slack channel to a supplier. No judgment required.

Today:
- Airtable stores the master data ($20/month)
- Zapier routes 4 core workflows ($49/month)
- ChatGPT drafts the follow-up emails ($20/month)

Total ops stack: $300/month. The $72K coordinator salary is gone.

This works because I spent three weeks documenting every decision I had in my head. Once it's in a system, software can execute it. Forever.

The judgment calls in my business? Those still need me. But the routing? That's plumbing. Build it once.

⚡ The question isn't 'who do I hire next?' The question is 'what am I still routing by hand?'

Map your decision tree. Then build the system that runs it.

So I mapped every task. 80% was just moving data. Airtable stores it. Zapier moves it. ChatGPT writes the emails. Total: $300/month.

You don't need headcount. You need a decision tree that runs without you.

youtube_shorts.title: I run $2M a year with zero employees
youtube_shorts.description:
The exact $300/month stack that replaced a $72K/year ops coordinator.

Stack breakdown:
- Airtable ($20/mo): Master data for orders, suppliers, and refunds
- Zapier ($49/mo): 4 workflows — order routing, refund flagging, supplier alerts, review requests
- ChatGPT ($20/mo): First draft of every supplier email and customer response

Total: $300/month including a couple of Shopify apps.

If you're a solo founder thinking about hiring — watch this first. You might just need a system, not a salary.

#solofounder #ecommerce #automation #airtable #zapier #systembuilding
